provenance.title: A Quantum Approach to Synthetic Minority Oversampling Technique (SMOTE)
affiliations: Centre for Quantum Software and Information, University of Technology Sydney, NSW, Australia
Bikash's Quantum (OPC) Pvt. Ltd., Balindi, Mohanpur, WB, India
provenance.abstract: The paper proposes the Quantum-SMOTE method, a novel solution that uses quantum computing techniques to solve the prevalent problem of class imbalance in machine learning datasets. Quantum-SMOTE generates synthetic data points using quantum processes such as swap tests and quantum rotation, enabling synthetic instance generation without relying on neighbor proximity.
key_claims: Quantum-SMOTE generates synthetic minority-class data via quantum rotation and swap test—no KNN or Euclidean distances required.
A compact swap test circuit dramatically reduces qubit count: log2(M)+1 qubits for M-dimensional data (5 qubits for 32 features vs 65 classically).
Hyperparameters (rotation angle, minority_percentage, split_factor) give fine-grained control over synthetic data generation.
Applied to TelecomChurn dataset; Random Forest F1 improves from 0.575 (no SMOTE) to 0.835 at 50% synthetic minority.
Low-depth circuits reduce sensitivity to quantum noise and decoherence.

architecture.description: The Quantum-SMOTE pipeline is a hybrid classical-quantum algorithm composed of five sequential stages: data preprocessing, K-Means clustering, compact swap test, quantum rotation, and synthetic data aggregation.

components.name: DataPreprocessor
components.role: Cleans raw tabular dataset: drop nulls, remove irrelevant columns, type conversion, label encoding, one-hot encoding, MinMax scaling.
components.input: Raw CSV tabular dataset (TelecomChurn: 7043 rows x 21 cols)
components.output: Preprocessed feature matrix X: [N, F] float, F=32 after OHE; target vector y: [N] binary

components.name: KMeansClusterer
components.role: Segments dataset into K clusters (min K=3 recommended). Assigns each sample to a cluster; computes cluster centroids.
components.output: Cluster labels: [N] int; cluster centroids: [K, F] float

components.name: PrepSwapTest
components.role: Encodes a minority data point (M) and cluster centroid (C) into quantum states phi and psi via amplitude embedding.
components.output: phi: [2] float (norm-derived 2D state vector); psi: [2F] float (interleaved normalized components)

components.name: CompactSwapTest
components.role: Estimates inner product <M|C> between minority data point and centroid quantum states, yielding swap_test_probability and angular_distance.
components.output: swap_test_probability: scalar float; angular_distance: scalar float (radians)
components.qubit_count: n = log2(M) + 1 where M = F (feature count after OHE); for F=32 → 6 qubits total (1 ancilla + 5 data qubits)
components.circuit_structure: Ancilla |0> → Pauli-X → H → Controlled-SWAP(ancilla, q2[0], q2[1]) → H → Measure ancilla

components.name: AngleCalculator
components.role: Derives rotation angle from angular_distance using split_factor to control proximity of synthetic point to source.
components.input: angular_distance: scalar float; split_factor: int; loop_ctr (iteration index for angle increment): int

components.name: QuantumRotator
components.role: Initializes a quantum circuit with normalized minority data point as statevector, applies RX gates to all qubits with computed angle, returns real part of output statevector as synthetic data point.
components.input: normalized minority data point: [F] float; angle: scalar float
components.output: synthetic_data_point: [F] float (real part of output statevector)
components.circuit_structure: Initialize |psi> = normalized minority vector → RX(angle) on all qubits → Simulate → Extract real(statevector)
components.notes: NO entangling gates (CNOT, ZZ, etc.) used—intentional design choice to avoid unwanted rotation artifacts.

components.name: QuantumSMOTE_Orchestrator
components.role: Top-level loop that processes each cluster, determines how many synthetic samples are needed to reach target minority percentage, calls PrepSwapTest → CompactSwapTest → AngleCalculator → QuantumRotator for each minority sample per cluster, and accumulates synthetic DataFrame.
components.input: Preprocessed data with cluster labels, target_pct (30/40/50), cluster_centroids: [K, F]
components.output: syn_dataframe: [N_syn, F] float — synthetic minority samples
components.operation_type: Classical orchestration loop with quantum sub-calls

hyperparameters.angle_increment: 0.0174533 radians per synthetic loop iteration (≈1 degree)

components.name: ClassificationEvaluator
components.role: Trains Random Forest and Logistic Regression on original + synthetic data; evaluates with confusion matrix, accuracy, F1, PR-AUC, ROC-AUC.

pipeline_flow: DataPreprocessor → KMeansClusterer → [for each cluster] PrepSwapTest → CompactSwapTest → AngleCalculator → QuantumRotator → syn_dataframe → ClassificationEvaluator

equations.name: Standard Swap Test Probability
equations.role: Relates measurement probability to inner product magnitude; derivation baseline.

equations.name: Inner Product from Swap Test
equations.role: Inverts EQ1 to recover inner product.

equations.name: Swap Test Probability (measurement definition)
equations.role: Operational definition used in Algorithm 4 to compute swap_test_probability from shot counts.

equations.name: Amplitude Encoding of Centroid and Minority
equations.role: Encodes classical vectors into quantum amplitude states.

equations.name: Compact Swap Test State Preparation
equations.role: Defines the two registers for the compact swap test circuit.

equations.name: Inner Product from Compact Swap Test
equations.role: Key result: recovers inner product <M|C> from measurement probability P_0 using compact circuit.

equations.name: Euclidean Distance from Swap Test
equations.role: Shows swap test also encodes Euclidean distance; referenced from prior literature.

equations.name: Angular Distance
equations.role: Converts swap_test_probability to angular distance in radians; used as basis for rotation angle.

equations.name: Rotation Angle Logic (three-branch)

variables.sf: split_factor hyperparameter (controls how small the rotation is)

equations.role: Determines per-sample rotation angle; smaller sf → larger rotation → synthetic point farther from source.

equations.name: PrepSwapTest Vector Construction
equations.pseudocode: norm1 = sqrt(sum(dp1^2)); norm2 = sqrt(sum(dp2^2)); Z = round(norm1^2 + norm2^2); phi = [round(norm1/sqrt(Z), 3), -round(norm2/sqrt(Z), 3)]; psi = interleaved [dp1[i]/(norm1*sqrt(2)), dp2[i]/(norm2*sqrt(2))] for all i
equations.role: Classical encoding step that builds phi and psi inputs for the compact swap test circuit.

equations.name: Qubit Count Formula
equations.role: Key efficiency claim: scales logarithmically with feature count.

tensors.name: X (feature matrix)
tensors.shape_notes: N = number of samples; F = 32 (after OHE + MinMax scaling on TelecomChurn)

tensors.name: psi (quantum state)
tensors.role: intermediate — input to CompactSwapTest; psi[2i] = C component, psi[2i+1] = M component

tensors.name: angle
tensors.role: intermediate output of AngleCalculator; input to QuantumRotator

tensors.name: statevector
tensors.role: intermediate — output of QuantumRotator Qiskit simulation

tensors.name: synthetic_data_point
tensors.dtype: float64 (real part of statevector, first F components)
tensors.role: output of QuantumRotator; one row of syn_dataframe

tensors.name: syn_dataframe
tensors.role: final output of QuantumSMOTE_Orchestrator; merged with original data for classification

tensor_semantics.notes: Statevector extraction: after RX rotation, the real part of the statevector is taken as the synthetic data point. The length 2^n may exceed F; paper uses the first F meaningful components (loop_ctr controls qubit mapping).

training_pipeline.quantum_backend: Qiskit Aer statevector_simulator (no real hardware used)
training_pipeline.shots: Not explicitly stated for swap test; statevector simulation is deterministic — shots are inferred from counts in Algorithm 4. Likely 1024 (Qiskit default).
data_preprocessing_steps: Convert categorical columns (PhoneService, MultipleLines, InternetService, OnlineSecurity, OnlineBackup, DeviceProtection, TechSupport, StreamingTV, StreamingMovies, Contract, PaperlessBilling, PaymentMethod, gender, SeniorCitizen, Partner, Dependents) to category dtype
Convert TotalCharges, tenure, MonthlyCharges to float
Correlation analysis to drop multicollinear / low-relevance features
One-hot encoding of remaining categoricals → F=32 final features
MinMax scaling of continuous features (TotalCharges, tenure, MonthlyCharges) to [0, 1]

clustering.purpose: Segment population before synthetic generation; centroid used as reference for angular distance

smote_hyperparameters.split_factor: Tested at 2, 5, 10 (X/Z rotation), 100 (Y rotation); paper uses split_factor=5 for main experiments (inferred)
smote_hyperparameters.rotation_axis: X (RX gates); Y and Z also tested experimentally
smote_hyperparameters.normalization: NormalizeArray function; scales array so sum of squares = 1 (unit vector)

classification_training.framework: scikit-learn (inferred from standard Python ML ecosystem)
classification_training.train_test_split: Not specified in paper — inferred ~80/20 from result row counts
classification_training.random_forest_params: Default scikit-learn params (n_estimators not stated; class_weight not stated)
classification_training.logistic_regression_params: Default scikit-learn params (solver not stated; C not stated; max_iter not stated)
classification_training.cv_strategy: Not mentioned — single train/test split used

training_pipeline.notes: This paper does not train a neural network. 'Training' here refers to fitting classical ML classifiers. The quantum component is solely for synthetic data generation (no quantum gradient descent or variational optimization).

dataset.name: Telco Customer Churn
dataset.class_distribution: Imbalanced; churn (minority) approx 26%, no-churn (majority) approx 74% — exact split not stated in paper

evaluation_protocol.baseline: Each model trained on imbalanced data without SMOTE serves as the baseline.
evaluation_protocol.compute: IBM Quantum Experience platform; Qiskit Aer statevector simulator. No GPU/CPU specs stated.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Qiskit version: qiskit-aer + qiskit-terra (pre-2.0 API based on circuit.initialize() and execute() syntax shown in pseudocode)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Algorithms 4 and 6 use legacy Qiskit API (execute(), get_statevector()). Modern Qiskit uses Sampler/Estimator primitives.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Number of shots for swap test: 1024 (Qiskit default)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper says 'set up quantum simulator, execute circuit, collect result' but does not specify shots. For statevector simulation, shots are effectively infinite (exact probabilities). Algorithm 4 references counts.get('0') and counts.get('1'), suggesting shot-based simulation is also used.
alternatives: Use statevector directly (shots=None for exact probabilities)

implementation_assumptions.basis: Inferred from confusion matrix row/column totals in figures; paper does not state split ratio or stratification strategy.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Random Forest: n_estimators=100, default scikit-learn params
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper does not state RF hyperparameters; scikit-learn default is 100 estimators.
scikit-learn defaults; paper does not specify.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: Feature selection step removes correlated/irrelevant features leaving exactly 32 final features
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper explicitly states 32 columns post feature-selection + OHE. Exact features removed not listed.
alternatives: Could vary if different correlation threshold chosen

implementation_assumptions.basis: scikit-learn defaults; paper only states K=3 and recommends minimum 3 clusters.

implementation_assumptions.assumption: The synthetic data points are generated using ONLY the minority class samples (Churn=1), not the majority class
implementation_assumptions.basis: Explicit in paper and Algorithm 7 (minority_count_in_cluster is computed separately).

implementation_assumptions.assumption: The real part of the output statevector (length 2^n) is truncated/reshaped to match original feature dimensionality F
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper states 'new_data_point = Real(statevector)' and uses loop_ctr = round(F/n + add) to control this; exact reshaping logic is ambiguous.
alternatives: Use only n qubits that span F dimensions

implementation_assumptions.assumption: split_factor=5 is used for the main experiment (Telecom results)
implementation_assumptions.basis: Paper shows split_factor experiments (2, 5, 10, 100) in Section 3 but does not explicitly state which value was used in Section 4.

ambiguities.description: The paper states 'new_data_point = Real(statevector)' but the statevector has length 2^n_qubits which may not equal F=32. The loop_ctr variable (= round(F/n + add)) is passed to create_syn_data but how it is used to map 2^n → F is not fully described.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Take real(statevector[:F]) — first F components after RX rotation.

ambiguities.description: Statevector simulator gives exact amplitudes (no sampling noise). But Algorithm 4 uses counts.get('0') and counts.get('1'), implying shot-based simulation. Reconciling these is ambiguous.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Use shot-based simulation (shots=1024) for compatibility with Algorithm 4 pseudocode.
alternatives: Use statevector directly and compute probabilities analytically

ambiguities.description: Paper performs correlation-based feature selection but does not list which features are retained or the exact correlation threshold.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Use threshold=0.8 for multi-collinearity and drop features with correlation < 0.05 to target.
alternatives: Retain all 32 OHE features without explicit correlation drop

ambiguities.location: Table 1, train accuracy for Logistic Regression without SMOTE
ambiguities.description: Table 1 shows train_acc=0.797 and test_acc=0.766 for LR without SMOTE. Section 4.1.6 text says 'test accuracy of 0.796622' which matches train, not test. Minor inconsistency.
ambiguities.primary_assumption: Trust Table 1 values as authoritative; text description has a typo.
